name: A Face in the Crowd
description: Temporarily conceal inhuman appearance under the guise of a very average looking human being (P.B. 1D4+8). Temporarily loses all Morphus abilities (flight, weapon limbs, claws, etc.), but stats remain unchanged.

enhancement.summary: 5 P.P.E. for each minute of continued duration.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. to activate plus 5 P.P.E. for each additional minute.

morphusTablePrerequisites: Must have at least one monstrous or alien trait/feature
limitations.otherLimitations: Temporarily loses all Morphus abilities (flight, weapon limbs, claws, horns, and Horror Factor); attributes, P.P.E., S.D.C., and Hit Points remain unchanged. Does not mask supernatural aura — Hounds, Hunters, and similar beings recognize and can hunt the user. Will not work on attractive or completely human-looking Nightbane (e.g. Kens and Barbies).

prerequisites.summary: At least one monstrous or alien Morphus trait from any appropriate table. Requires G.M. confirmation — attractive or fully human-looking Morphus forms do not qualify.

name: Abduction
description: A single target is whisked away by dark energies to a limbo-like pocket dimension. No harm comes to the target, but they are stuck unless they can teleport.

enhancement.summary: +6 P.P.E. for each additional melee round.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. for a one melee round duration, plus 6 P.P.E. for each additional melee round.

ranges.summary: 90 feet (27 m) + 10 feet (3 m) per level of experience

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds) per level of experience.

save.summary: Roll 15 or higher on a D20 with P.E. bonuses to resist abduction.

prerequisites.summary: One or more characteristics from the Alien Shape or Extraterrestrial table.

name: Beguiling Beauty
description: Upon activating this Talent, the unnatural good looks of the user become, quite literally, stunning. Anyone who looks directly at the Beguiling Beauty must make a save vs magic roll of 12 or higher. Any person who saves is unaffected, but those who fail are awestruck at the mere sight of the Nightbane and blissfully unaware of anything other than the character's beauty and what she or he is doing. Victims stand slack-jawed staring at the Beauty while a bank vault is emptied, prisoners escape, etc. After the individuals snap out of being beguiled, the only part of the experience they remember is the image of the Beguiling Beauty who so transfixed them.

ppe.notes: Nine P.P.E. per melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: Beguiling Beauty will not keep victims from reacting to attacks directed at them by anyone other than the Beguiling Beauty, but they only fight to defend themselves or to quickly take down their attacker so they can return to admiring the beauty. Any hostile or aggressive action on the part of the Nightbane who is the Beguiling Beauty immediately cancels this Talent's effects and those who were enthralled snap out of the enchantment as if waking from a dream.

ranges.summary: 8 foot (2.4 m) radius per level of the Nightbane's experience

duration.summary: Active while 9 P.P.E. is spent per melee round. Trance lasts for 1 minute (4 melee rounds) after the Talent lapses or the affected person is no longer within range.

save.summary: Save vs magic 12 or higher
save.effectsOnSuccessfulSave: Opponents not beguiled are still a bit distracted by the Beguiling Beauty and are -1 on all combat rolls and -5% on the performance of skills.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Victims stand slack-jawed, awestruck, and blissfully unaware of anything other than the character's beauty and what she or he is doing.

prerequisites.summary: At least one feature from the Unnatural Beauty, Angelic, or Superbeing table and experience level 6 or greater.

name: Beyond the Call
description: History is full of stories of relatively unremarkable individuals performing extraordinary feats of courage, endurance, strength, etc. With this Talent, a Nightbane can channel magical energy to accomplish similarly astonishing results. When activating Beyond the Call, the player selects ONE of the following traits to temporarily boost to a superhuman level: I.Q., Mental Endurance, Physical Strength, Physical Prowess, Physical Endurance, Speed, and/or S.D.C.

ppe.notes: Eight P.P.E. to select any one temporary boost described in the power modes.

ranges.summary: Self only

duration.summary: Two melee rounds (30 seconds)

prerequisites.summary: Level 4 or greater and one or more features from the Soldier (any), Superbeing, or Occupation table.

notes: Make sure all appropriate bonuses are applied for the duration of the selected boost.

name: Bloodbath
description: This grotesque power allows the Nightbane to use his own blood to suffocate victims. A stream of blood pours from the character and rushes into the victim's nose and throat.

prerequisites.summary: At least one characteristic from the Stigmata Table.

ppe.notes: Activating the Talent inflicts 1D6 S.D.C. points of damage on the user.

duration.summary: 1D4 melee rounds

damageModifiersNote: Inflicted at the end of each melee round.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Victim is at -2 on all combat actions and is unable to use spells, Talents or any skill that requires concentration.

save.summary: A successful save versus non-lethal poison will avoid damage for that round, but the penalties remain.
A victim unfamiliar with that attack must save versus a Horror Factor of 12 to avoid panicking. If panicked, the penalties for the victim are doubled.

secondarySave.summary: If panicked, the penalties are doubled.

conditionalPenaltyMultipliersOnFailure.notes: Combat penalties for the victim are doubled if panicked.

name: Choke
description: Enables the Nightbane to cause opponents to suddenly fail the next action he takes. Whether it is a combat roll to strike, parry, dodge, etc., a Perception Roll, a saving throw, skill roll, etc., the opponent who fails to save vs magic automatically fails his next action and suffers the consequences for it (if any).

ranges.summary: 100 feet (30.5 m) per level of experience and the target must be visible; line of sight.

duration.summary: Affects only the next action of one specific character, but failure could have lasting consequences (e.g. failing to save vs poison or mind control, crashing a vehicle, missing a Perception Roll and getting captured).

save.summary: Save vs magic 15 or better to save

notes: Targets who do save are not impacted by this magic. The user typically has no way of knowing for certain what a target's next action will be — guesswork, timing, and a bit of luck are often involved.

prerequisites.summary: At least one feature from the Athlete, Artisan, Hobbyist, Occupation, or Infernal table and level 3 or greater.

name: Commanding Presence
description: Temporarily increases M.A. by six points (minimum 22). On a successful Trust/Intimidate roll, observers must save or be completely swayed by the Nightbane. Cumulative with Animal Magnetism.

enhancement.summary: 6 P.P.E. for each two minutes thereafter.

ppe.notes: Twelve P.P.E. for the first two minutes, plus 6 P.P.E. for each additional two minutes.

limitations.otherLimitations: M.A. increased by six points (minimum 22). On a successful Trust/Intimidate roll, subjects must roll under their M.E. to resist. Cumulative with Animal Magnetism. Nightbane and other supernatural creatures are +3 to save.

save.summary: Subjects must roll under their M.E. attribute to save vs the Nightbane's Trust/Intimidate after a successful roll by the user.

prerequisites.summary: Only Nightbane with an Unearthly Beauty or Angel trait may select this Talent.

name: Contortionist
description: A brutal yet effective means of attacking and debilitating opponents, this Talent enables the user to violently manipulate the limbs of his victims. Upon activating Contortionist, the Nightbane dislocates one of his or her own limbs or digits. The target of the Talent then must attempt to save vs magic of 12 or better. If the save is unsuccessful, an arm, leg, tentacle, etc., is twisted and contorted out of socket. This attack does 2D6 damage and inhibits the use of that limb until medical attention is provided, or the damage is healed.

activationDamage.notes: The Nightbane dislocates one of his or her own limbs or digits.

limitations.otherLimitations: The user must have some kind of appendage or digit that can be dislocated.

ranges.summary: Touch or four feet (1.2 m) away; line of sight.

duration.summary: Instant. Penalties last until a successful First Aid or higher medical skill is used to reset the limb in the socket or damage is regenerated/healed.

save.summary: Save vs magic 12 or better; on failure, save vs pain 14 or better (using any M.E. bonuses) to remain conscious.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: An arm, leg, tentacle, etc., is twisted and contorted out of socket. This attack does 2D6 damage and inhibits the use of that limb until medical attention is provided, or the damage is healed. Most ordinary human victims pass out for 1D4 melee rounds (15–60 seconds) unless they save vs pain at 14 or better.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Even if the target remains conscious, the pain causes the loss of two melee attacks. Losing the use of one arm imposes a -3 penalty to strike, parry, disarm, and entangle, and prevents the use of weapons or tools with that limb. Losing the use of half of all legs drops Spd by 70% and imposes -6 to dodge. Losing use of a wing, fin, or other appendage prevents winged flight, reduces swimming Spd by 70%, inhibits special attacks, etc. (G.M. discretion).

notes: Can be used multiple times consecutively on the same limb to overcome a victim's supernatural healing rate (penalties last until all damage done to the limb by this Talent is regenerated). For lesser targets like normal humans, healing is likely to take 1D4 weeks unless psionic or magical healing is used.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one Morphus feature from the Unnatural Limbs, Victim, or Disproportion tables.

name: Corrupting Influence
description: Temporarily turning the Talent user into the archetypical 'silver-tongued devil,' the Corrupting Influence Talent makes victims more susceptible to suggestions relating to their darker nature. Such suggestions might play to a target's greed, jealousy, anger, lust for revenge, etc. For instance, a bullied teenager might be talked into attacking the bully, an ambitious gang-banger into skimming profits from his crew's drug sales, or a jealous wife into trying to kill her husband's coworker. Whatever the task the Talent user wants to accomplish, there must be a justifiable motive for the person affected to do it, which is likely to require some intelligence-gathering on the player's part. The assigned task can even be something the target normally wouldn't do as long as it isn't more than one alignment below their current alignment. Submitting to a Corrupting Influence does NOT make that character become an evil alignment — it means he was pushed to do something he normally would not. His alignment does not drop though he may feel guilt and regret. A Selfish or evil aligned character can just as likely be convinced to perform a good deed.

activationTiers.summary: Suggestion to target with an evil alignment
Suggestion to target with a selfish alignment
Suggestion to target with a good alignment

ppe.notes: Four P.P.E. per suggestion for evil targets, 8 for selfish, 12 for good.

limitations.otherLimitations: The Nightbane's own alignment must be Anarchist or evil. Those affected by this Talent can NOT be made to seriously injure themselves or a loved one. See description for alignment limits on assigned tasks.

ranges.summary: Touch or 10 feet (3 m) and must be able to hear the Talent user speak.

duration.summary: The Talent is only good for a single suggestion per use.

save.summary: Save vs magic; good alignment 12+, selfish 14+, evil 16+.

notes: The Nightbane can tell whether or not the Talent succeeded before making the suggestion. If the Talent user or a recognized ally attacks a person under the sway of Corrupting Influence, its effects are immediately negated the moment after the deed is done.

prerequisites.summary: Level 4 or greater, one or more features from the Infernal, Substance Abuse, or Unearthly Beauty table, and the Nightbane's own alignment must be Anarchist or evil.

name: Dark Dreams
description: This Talent has a handful of different uses involving nightmares and the manipulation of dreams. First, it can be used on a sleeping target to attempt to impose a nightmare. If the victim fails the saving throw, a nightmare with the chosen level of Horror Factor invades his dreams. The victim must then attempt to save vs the nightmare's Horror Factor or awaken within 1D4 melee rounds feeling robbed of any sleep previously attained — no restored Hit Points, S.D.C., P.P.E., or I.S.P. While this may not seem powerful at first glance, if used over a long period of time and even combined with other tactics, it can debilitate or even drive people insane. If the Talent user is capable of entering Dream Pools (Nightbane World Book One: Between the Shadows), Dark Dreams can be used to orchestrate a nightmare from within and even engage in dream combat — darkening or reshaping the Dream Pool, turning native dream constructs against the dreamer, and leveling Dream Manipulation attacks with a +3 bonus.

activationTiers.summary: Impose a nightmare (+1 P.P.E. per additional Horror Factor point)

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. to impose a nightmare (+1 per additional Horror Factor). Twelve P.P.E. per minute to darken a Dream Pool. Eight P.P.E. per minute to manipulate a dream construct. Two P.P.E. per dream attack.

limitations.otherLimitations: Can only cause or worsen nightmares — cannot end a nightmare, decrease its intensity, or give it a happy ending. Cannot be used in the Talent user's own Dream Pool. Only the dreamer and native dream constructs can be affected, except for dream attacks. Maximum Horror Factor for an imposed nightmare is 15; limit when darkening a Dream Pool is 17.

ranges.summary: Up to 3 feet (0.9 m) from the target when imposing nightmares. Affects a single target's Dream Pool.

duration.summary: 1D4 minutes for imposed nightmares; Dream Pool manipulation duration see P.P.E. costs.

save.summary: Resisting an imposed nightmare requires save vs magic 14 or better.
After failing the magic save, victim must save vs the nightmare's Horror Factor or awaken within 1D4 melee rounds with no sleep benefit.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Awaken within 1D4 melee rounds feeling robbed of any sleep previously attained — no restored Hit Points, S.D.C., P.P.E., or I.S.P.

save.summary: Dream construct resists Nightbane control with save vs magic 14 or better. Resisting Dream Pool manipulation uses dream combat rolls at the start of each melee round under the Talent user's influence.
save.notes: If the dreamer wrests control back, all capability to manipulate constructs and bonuses to dream attacks end.

notes: When darkening a Dream Pool, failed resistance gives the environment a Horror Factor of 5 plus the Talent user's experience level.

prerequisites.summary: Level 2 or higher and at least one feature from the Nightmare, Victim, Substance Abuse, or Mythical Creature table.

name: Dark Fathom
description: Aquatic Nightbane can use this Talent to temporarily change the water nearby to that of the darkest ocean depths. Inside the affected area, no light penetrates from the outside — even passive nightvision does not work, though flashlights, sonar, and other navigation aids still function. Magical or supernatural nightvision works at half the usual range. Natural buoyancy is reduced, preventing victims from simply floating back to the surface. Veteran divers must save vs Horror Factor 10; sea creatures and inexperienced divers save vs Horror Factor 15. Those who fail panic, swim in confused circles, and roll again each melee round until they regain rational action. Panicked victims are -4 to all combat rolls and Perception Rolls (double if blind) and cannot perform skills other than Swimming. Unprotected swimmers suffer hypothermia within 1D4 minutes; without oxygen, panicked characters drown in 10 seconds per P.E. point.

enhancement.summary: 4 P.P.E. for every additional minute sustained.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. to activate and 4 P.P.E. for every additional minute sustained.

ranges.summary: Up to 100 feet (30.5 m) away +10 feet (3 m) per level of experience.

areaOfEffect.summary: 20 foot (6.1 m) radius per level of experience.

duration.summary: See P.P.E. costs. Hypothermia penalties last 3D6×10 minutes after drying off (1D4×10 for supernatural creatures).

save.summary: Veteran divers save vs Horror Factor 10.
save.notes: Roll again every melee round until the victim regains rational action.

save.summary: Sea creatures and inexperienced divers save vs Horror Factor 15.
save.notes: Sea creatures almost always flee the dark waters as quickly as possible.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Panicked state: penalties double if blind; unable to perform skills other than Swimming. Hypothermia within 1D4 minutes for unprotected swimmers (-20% skills, -1 attack, -2 all combat rolls). Oxygen supply used twice as fast while panicked. Normal persons slip unconscious within 5D6+P.E. minutes; supernatural beings without cold protection take 4D4 minutes for hypothermia and 2D4×10+P.E. minutes before unconsciousness.

notes: Water temperature drops to 39°F (4°C). Without oxygen, panicked characters last 10 seconds per P.E. point before drowning.

prerequisites.summary: Level 3 or greater and at least one feature from an Aquatic Animal table or the Aquatic Biomech table.

name: Deus Ex Machina
description: The Nightbane can control machines from cars to microwaves, to lamps or weapons. One device can be controlled for every three points of I.Q. (e.g. I.Q. 13 controls four machines).

prerequisites.summary: At least one biomechanical characteristic.

enhancement.summary: 6 P.P.E. for each additional minute of control, or 20 P.P.E. for an additional 100 foot (30.5 m) radius of control.

ppe.notes: Four P.P.E. to activate for one minute of control, plus 6 P.P.E. per additional minute or 20 P.P.E. per additional 100 foot (30.5 m) radius.

areaOfEffect.summary: Control radius can be expanded by spending extra P.P.E.

areaScalingByPpeStep.stepDefinition: 20 P.P.E. for an additional 100 foot (30.5 m) radius of control.

duration.summary: One minute

objectsAffected.summary: One device can be controlled for every three points of I.Q. he has.

notes: While the Nightbane can control several machines, only one of them can be directly controlled on an action-by-action basis. The remaining mechanisms can be given a general order for the rest of the round. The character can also seize control of guns and explosive devices.

name: Diamond-Hide
description: Used to greatly increase the Talent user's resistance to damage for a time, turning them into a supernatural juggernaut capable of shrugging off a hail of attacks. This is especially useful for Nightbane who already have a Natural A.R., as it costs less to boost existing Natural A.R. than generating one from scratch. While Diamond-Hide is active, the skin of the user takes on a sheen like a rough-cut gemstone or volcanic glass. All normal features of Natural A.R. function as usual — only the numerical value is enhanced.

enhancement.summary: Five P.P.E. per each point of Natural A.R. added.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. per each point of Natural A.R. added.

limitations.otherLimitations: Maximum boost is 2 A.R. points per Nightbane level up to a cap of A.R. 18. Only affects the Nightbane — not equipment or armor. Magical and psionic attacks ignore any boost (spells like Call Lightning, Talents like Shadow Blast, psionics like Pyrokinesis use the original A.R.).

ranges.summary: Self only

duration.summary: One minute

prerequisites.summary: Level 7 or greater and at least one feature from the Mineral, Mythical Creature, or Artificial Appearance table.

name: Divine Wrath
description: In an impressive display of supernatural power, the user of Divine Wrath appears to call down devastating retribution on anyone unfortunate enough to be in the area of effect. Once activated, the Nightbane becomes wreathed in a halo of light or fire and appears to float weightlessly into the air. Maximum speed is unchanged; floating height is 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience. By pointing or gesturing at a nearby area, a rain of destruction is called down from on high to strike any being caught within. Whether bolts of lightning, rain of fire, shafts of searing light, or something similar, damage and effects are always the same. Any target struck — or physically striking or grappling the Nightbane while wrapped in the halo — takes alignment-based damage. Targets may try to dodge; there is no saving throw. The area affected can be changed each time the Nightbane attacks.

limitations.otherLimitations: Typically used in dire circumstances. Can only be used once every four melee rounds (60 seconds). While using the Talent the Nightbane can use no other Talents, spells, or abilities and can only attempt to dodge incoming attacks (use normal bonuses). Uses up ALL the Nightbane's attacks. Targets may dodge; there is no saving throw.

ranges.summary: Up to 500 feet (152.4 m) away +50 feet (15.2 m) per level of experience.

areaOfEffect.summary: 10 foot (3 m) diameter of effect per level of experience.

duration.summary: One melee round (15 seconds); four energy blasts from Divine Wrath in that melee round.

bonusDamageVsNightlandsDenizens.summary: Triple damage against demons, Nightlords, their avatars and all evil creatures from the Nightlands!

notes: Double damage to supernatural beings and magical creatures. Triple damage against demons, Nightlords, their avatars, and all evil creatures from the Nightlands. The Talent user is +5 to strike with this onslaught but only rolls once per melee attack.

prerequisites.summary: Level 7 or greater and at least one feature from the Angelic, Fantasy, or Ancient Warrior table.

name: Epic Proportions
description: Enables the Nightbane to temporarily grow to a massive size, up to 30 feet (9.1 m). Bonuses: temporarily double the Talent-user's weight and add 100 S.D.C. per every 10 feet (3 m) of height (Hit Points unchanged); +10 P.S. (adjust punch/kick/stomp damage) and +10 Spd. Penalties: -1D6 P.P.; all combat bonuses are halved if P.P. falls below 8 and negated entirely at P.P. 2. Horror Factor increases by one per 10 feet of height up to a maximum of 18.

enhancement.summary: Fifteen P.P.E. per 10 feet (3 m) of additional height.

ppe.notes: Fifteen P.P.E. per 10 feet (3 m) of additional height (up to 30 feet / 9.1 m).

mutuallyExclusive.summary: No other Talent may be used by the character while Epic Proportions is in effect.

ranges.summary: Self

duration.summary: One minute (4 melee rounds) per level of experience.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Temporarily double weight; add 100 S.D.C. per 10 feet (3 m) of height. -1D6 P.P. All combat bonuses halved if P.P. falls below 8; negated entirely at P.P. 2. Horror Factor +1 per 10 feet of height (maximum 18).

notes: The bigger and scarier the character becomes, the more likely enemies (and mistaken allies) will attack the giant.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one feature from the Mythical Creature, Dinosaur, Nightmare, or Sci-Fi table.

name: Fan Club
description: In much the same way that god-like beings can call upon worshipers to increase reserves of magical energy, a Nightbane with this Talent can draw strength from an ethereal fan club — teammates or Faction members (PCs and NPCs), and anyone rooting for him or for the downfall of his opponent (such as a Nightlord or minions). The Fan Club Talent does not take P.P.E. from teammates or bystanders; it generates P.P.E. from their focus and emotions. When activated, low chanting or cheering is heard near the user and the Nightbane feels inspired, gaining a mystical energy bump.

limitations.otherLimitations: A maximum of 27 P.P.E. may become available every melee round while this Talent is activated to supplement the user's own.

ranges.summary: Self, or other by touch.

duration.summary: 1D4 melee rounds +1 melee round per level of experience.

temporaryResourcePools.summary: Each melee round the user needs to expend P.P.E., the cheering grows louder and 2D6+15 P.P.E. is available for activating other Talents or casting spells.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: Temporary +1 on all saving throws and +20 to save vs coma/death for the duration.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and a feature from the Athlete, Artisan, or Superbeing table.

name: Forbidding Woods
description: Radiates an aura of fear and confusion over a large area of woodland. Trees seem threatening, shadows deepen, and victims must save vs Horror Factor 16 or flee.

enhancement.summary: 7 P.P.E. per additional 100 foot (30.5 m) radius of woods affected.

ppe.notes: Seven P.P.E. per 100 foot (30.5 m) radius of woodland affected.

limitations.otherLimitations: Requires moderate to dense stands of living trees (forest, city park, jungle, bamboo grove, etc.). Failed save vs Horror Factor 16: victims flee, are confused/disoriented, and may get lost (Land Navigation or Navigation at -50%) until the Talent elapses.

duration.summary: 30 seconds (2 melee rounds) per level of experience.

save.summary: Save vs Horror Factor 16 or immediately attempt to flee in fear.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Plant Life characteristic.

name: Ghost Ship
description: Gives the user the ability to summon a supernatural conveyance in the form of a large, ghostly sailing vessel roughly the size of a Corsair/large cutter — approximately 80 feet (24.4 m) long and 20 feet (6.1 m) wide. Thirty to fifty passengers plus 40 tons of cargo can be carried; the Nightbane Talent user is the only crew needed and controls every aspect of the ship. Aesthetics may vary, but abilities are the same: 50 S.D.C. per level, sails seeming to blow in the wind, and like the Flying Dutchman it floats silently through air or water with Prowl 50%. Maximum altitude 1,000 feet (305 m); 60 mph (96 kph) at full sail without wind, 90 mph (144 kph) on a ley line or within a ley line triangle. A fog accompanies the ship; at 20 mph (32 kph) or slower the fog envelops the entire vessel (+30% to Prowl). Transport only — no weapon capabilities.

enhancement.summary: Thirty P.P.E. for every additional 20 minutes of travel.

ppe.notes: Thirty P.P.E. to activate and for every additional 20 minutes of travel. No additional P.P.E. needed to sustain the ship if it stays within one mile (1.6 km) of a ley line.

limitations.otherLimitations: The user must remain onboard the ship at all times. If he leaves the ship the Talent is cancelled and the vessel vanishes.

ranges.summary: Self

duration.summary: 20 minutes per 30 P.P.E. expended; sustained three times as long on a ley line. Fog maintained indefinitely while the Talent remains in effect.

createdObjects.name: Ghostly Sailing Vessel
createdObjects.sdc: 50 S.D.C. per level of the Talent user

conveyance.flightCapabilities: Floats silently through the air or over water; appears to sail on a small cloud of fog.

skillProficiencies.notes: +30% to Prowl when moving at 20 mph (32 kph) or slower (fog envelops the ship).

environmentalAmplifiers.environmentSummary: Ley line or ley line triangle
environmentalAmplifiers.effectSummary: Duration sustained three times as long; top speed increased to 90 mph (144 kph). No additional P.P.E. needed to sustain the ship within one mile (1.6 km) of a ley line.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one feature from the Ancient Warrior, Fantasy, or Undead table.

name: Gorgon's Touch
description: Physical touch turns the target temporarily into stone. Failed saves temporarily drain P.P., P.E., and Spd by 20%. Once attributes are reduced to 1, the victim is completely petrified.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. to activate for one full melee round in which the Nightbane's touch can turn a target to stone.

limitations.otherLimitations: Touch attack only — no punch/claw/P.S. damage. Penetrates clothing and most armor; modern full body armor requires beating the A.R. on a strike roll. Petrified victims are unaware of events (blackout), weight is doubled, and cannot be destroyed.

ranges.summary: Touch

duration.summary: Gorgon's Touch lasts one melee round or until the victim is turned to stone. Petrification lasts one minute (4 melee rounds) per level of the Nightbane.

save.summary: Save of 14 or better with P.E. bonuses. Failed cumulative touches reduce P.P., P.E., and Spd by 20% each; victim turns to stone when attributes fail to save or P.P./P.E. reach 1.

prerequisites.summary: At least one characteristic from the Mineral table.

name: Guardian Angel
description: Selects one or more targets for mystical protection (up to two per level). A ghostly image of the Nightbane superimposes over each warded individual; damage directed at them transfers to the Guardian Angel (S.D.C. first, then Hit Points) until the Nightbane drops to 15 Hit Points.

ppe.notes: Four P.P.E. to activate. Protection lasts until cancelled or the Nightbane's S.D.C. is depleted and Hit Points fall to 15.

prerequisites.summary: Only Aberrant, Unprincipled, Scrupulous, or Principled alignment. No Morphus table prerequisite.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only available to Aberrant, Unprincipled, Scrupulous, or Principled alignments. Line of sight required. Can protect up to two people per level of experience. Aura ends when the Nightbane reaches 15 Hit Points.

ranges.summary: Line of sight

duration.summary: Until the Nightbane cancels the Talent or S.D.C. is depleted and Hit Points are reduced to 15.

name: Hatred's Mirror
description: Typically developed by Nightbane who know what it is to be ostracized and abused, this powerful Talent turns aside any attack born of anger, hatred, or other ill intent. For the duration, any opponent with foul motives who attacks the Talent user discovers the true price of hatred — damage that would normally be dealt to the victim is instead inflicted upon the aggressor. The attack type does not matter: punch, psychic attack, spell, or Talent. If the user is targeted, Hatred's Mirror redirects effects to the point of origin; area effects center on whoever launched them. Spells, psionics, and Talents like Domination may simply fail; the attacker whose magic is turned on him may still save at -2. Some redirected attacks go unnoticed — especially against opponents in blind rage or blood frenzy.

ppe.notes: Sixteen P.P.E. per melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only malicious attacks are redirected. If the aggressor is simply trying to restrain the Nightbane, protect others, or otherwise has good intentions behind any offense, it is not redirected. The Talent user cannot make any kind of attack against anyone while this Talent is active or it is immediately canceled.

ranges.summary: Self

duration.summary: One melee round.

reflectionMechanics.triggerCondition: Any attack born of anger, hatred, or other ill intent directed at the user.
reflectionMechanics.notes: Area-of-effect attacks center on the launcher. Some spells/psionics/Talents (e.g. Domination) simply fail; the attacker may save vs his own magic at -2. For the first redirected attack, the attacker rolls Perception — below 12 (higher for enraged, insane, or slow-witted foes) the self-inflicted damage goes unnoticed. Continue rolling each attack until the aggressor realizes his attacks only hurt himself.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one feature from the Unusual Facial Feature, Alien Shape, or Disproportion table.

name: Hysterical
description: Humor has long been part of human interaction, and those who can make others laugh possess a special kind of power. The Hysterical Talent takes humor and laughter into the supernatural realm — imbuing an object or the user with the ability to cause uncontrolled bouts of hysterical laughter. On self, the character must do something funny (silly face, jig, joke, pratfall, etc.). Enchanted objects must look funny or be used comically (e.g. a silly mustache drawn on a portrait). Anyone who sees or hears the humor must save vs magic 15 or higher or succumb to a laughing fit — loss of motor control, chest and throat pain, red face, and difficulty breathing.

limitations.otherLimitations: Victims can only be affected once per 12-hour period. Only intelligent beings with a relatively human sense of humor. Nightlords, Night Princes and Princesses, Doppelgangers, Guardians, and similar beings with human humor sensibilities are susceptible. Anyone who smiles, chuckles, or laughs at the antics may become hysterical. If a victim is attacked in any way while under the influence, the effect and penalties end in 1D6 seconds.

ranges.summary: Self or an object/prop up to 10 feet (3 m) away. The power then affects anyone that sees or hears the humor.

duration.summary: Latent up to one day per level of the Talent user. Once active, affects people for 5D6 minutes. Failed-save penalties last 1D6 minutes +1 minute per level.

save.summary: Save vs magic 15 or higher
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Uncontrolled laughing fit: last initiative, attacks per melee reduced by half, -5 to all combat rolls, -50% to skills, loss of motor control, chest/throat pain, difficulty breathing. Unable to defend against the Talent user's first attack.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Face turns red; difficulty breathing. If attacked by the Talent user, unable to defend against the first attack. Any attack on the victim ends the effect in 1D6 seconds.

prerequisites.summary: Level 4 or greater and at least one feature from the Clown, Artisan, or Artificial Appearance table.

name: Kill Switch
description: Mimics the effects of an EMP, temporarily shutting down all unprotected electronic devices and erasing unprotected magnetic/hard drive data within a 100 foot radius.

ppe.notes: Twelve P.P.E. for instant shutdown of unprotected electronics within range.

limitations.otherLimitations: 100 foot (30.5 m) radius; the Nightbane's own gear is unaffected. Hardened military electronics, sophisticated alarms, and surveillance systems continue to operate. Also erases unprotected hard-drive, disk, and magnetic-tape data. 01–40% chance to knock out most automobile/truck electrical systems.

ranges.summary: 100 foot (30.5 m) radius around the Nightbane

duration.summary: Shuts down affected electronics for 1D6+1 minutes.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Biomechanical characteristic.

name: Legionnaire
description: Nightbane with this Talent create shadowy copies of their Morphus form to even the odds when outnumbered. Copies have half the user's combat bonuses, half attacks per melee, skills at half ability, 30% of the creator's S.D.C., and no Hit Points. P.S. is halved (minimum 10) but remains Supernatural. Natural A.R. does NOT apply to copies. Copies defend the user and nearby associates or follow simple commands (one action per command). Destroyed at 0 S.D.C. they fade into shadow; dispellable by Anti-Magic Cloud or Anti-Arcane aura; take double damage from light-based attacks. Fooled by illusions; Hypnotic Suggestion, Telepathy, and similar mind-targeting effects fail. Prowl 70% in shadows, emit no heat, and do not trigger pressure plates.

ppe.notes: Fifteen P.P.E. per each individual copy created.

limitations.otherLimitations: Copies cannot speak, cast spells, or activate Talents. For every pair of copies created, subtract one melee attack/action from the user. If reduced to no attacks/actions the Nightbane can only direct copies and cannot create more until one or more is dismissed or destroyed.

ranges.summary: The copies must stay within 10 feet (3 m) per level of experience of the user.

duration.summary: One minute at level 5, plus one additional minute for each level after 5.

createdObjects.name: Shadow Copy
createdObjects.notes: Half combat bonuses and attacks; skills at half ability; P.S. halved (minimum 10, Supernatural); no Hit Points; no Natural A.R. Tripwires, cameras, and motion detectors detect them. Double damage from light-based attacks.

notes: Dispelled by Anti-Magic Cloud or Anti-Arcane aura. Mind-targeting spells/psionics fail; illusions can fool copies.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one Morphus feature from the Ancient Warrior or Artificial Appearance table.

name: Lend a Hand
description: Temporarily lend an Unnatural or Weapon Limb to another mortal or Nightbane. The recipient gains a duplicate limb (or a third appendage) with the lender's physical characteristics — claws, supernatural strength, tentacles, etc. Does not transfer spells, Talents, or other abilities beyond that limb.

enhancement.summary: 3 P.P.E. for every additional melee round.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. for one melee round, plus 3 P.P.E. per additional melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: Typically limited to arms, hands, tails, or an extra limb. The lender retains their own Morphus limb.

ranges.summary: Touch

duration.summary: One melee round per activation, extendable by additional P.P.E.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Unnatural Limb or Biomechanical Morphus feature.

name: Living Nightmare
description: Upon activating this Talent, the Nightbane becomes the epitome of fear. Each person within the area must roll save vs magic or save vs illusion of 14 or higher. Those who fail see the Talent user change into whatever each individual fears most, imposing an effective Horror Factor of 14+1D4 per victim. Victims then save against that Horror Factor without bonuses — failure means they will do almost anything to escape, even trampling others. Unlike normal Horror Factor, the first attack on a terrified victim does NOT automatically succeed; affected targets are +3 to dodge and want only to get away.

limitations.otherLimitations: Anyone affected may re-roll the save vs Horror Factor at the beginning of each new melee round.

ranges.summary: Self, but targets are affected up to 20 feet (6.1 m) away per level of experience; line of sight required.

duration.summary: 30 seconds (2 melee rounds) per level of experience. Victims remain affected until they flee from sight or roll a successful save vs Horror Factor.

save.summary: Save vs magic or illusion 14 or higher; on failure, save vs Horror Factor 14+1D4 without bonuses.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: The Talent user appears as each victim's greatest fear (Horror Factor 14+1D4). Failed Horror Factor save: victim flees, willing to trample others to escape.

notes: First attack on a terrified victim does not automatically succeed. Affected victims are +3 to dodge the Nightbane.

prerequisites.summary: One or more features from the Nightmare, Stigmata, or Alien Shape table.

name: Lord/Lady of the Wild
description: The Nightbane gains mastery over any animal species he/she is associated with (Animal Control). A wolf-like Nightbane controls wolves and canines; a bird-like character controls all birds, and so on.

prerequisites.summary: At least one characteristic from the Animal Form Table.

limitations.otherLimitations: Control is limited to the appropriate species.

activationTiers.name: Calling animals
activationTiers.effectDescription: Calling animals of the appropriate species from up to a one mile (1.6 km) radius per level of experience.

rangeOverride.summary: One mile (1.6 km) per level of experience

activationTiers.name: Minor, non-dangerous actions
activationTiers.effectDescription: Like making noise, hiding, fluttering about per large animal or per group of up to ten small animals.

activationTiers.name: Dangerous actions
activationTiers.effectDescription: Threatening humans, bite/attack, or behavior that goes against the animal's normal behavior per large animal or per group of up to ten small animals.

activationTiers.name: Suicidal actions
activationTiers.effectDescription: Recklessly attacking humans or supernatural beings, intercepting vehicles, fight to the death per large animal or per group of up to ten small animals.

ppe.notes: Activation cost varies by command type. Summon range one mile (1.6 km) per level; command range 500 feet (152 m).

ranges.summary: 500 feet (152 m) to control/command.

duration.summary: 15 minutes.

name: Master Stroke
description: Many artists claim to fall into a certain state of mind at peak performance — being 'hot,' 'in the zone,' or 'creating up a storm.' Master Stroke (In the Zone) taps this phenomenon to boost any one skill to phenomenal quality. Recipients feel focused and refreshed; work completes at breakneck speed (half normal time) without quality loss — successful rolls yield impeccable work. Downsides: total focus (-10 Perception, no breaks); if roused, half attacks and no combat bonuses; exhausting aftermath (often 10+ hours sleep and a large meal). Repeated use risks psychological addiction — unscrupulous Nightbane have used it to enthrall artists, inventors, and politicians.

enhancement.summary: One P.P.E. per percentage point of the skill bonus imposed.

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per percentage point of skill bonus (maximum +5% per level of the Talent user).

limitations.otherLimitations: Maximum bonus +5% per level of the Talent user. Does not work on combat skills (hand to hand, W.P.s) or special abilities like track by scent — only skills. Anyone other than the user can only be affected once per day. Unwilling targets save vs magic 12 or better. G.M.s may reserve level-related skill growth for skills used with this Talent.

ranges.summary: Self, or other by touch.

duration.summary: Varies by task; 24 hours maximum.

save.summary: Each time an individual is affected, Mental Endurance saving throw 12 or better.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: -10% penalty to the augmented skill for 2D4 days. Three consecutive failures cause addiction: -20% to skills previously augmented by Master Stroke and -5% to all others.

save.summary: Unwilling targets may save vs magic 12 or better to resist.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Worker does not stop to eat, sleep, or take bathroom breaks while focused. If roused, half attacks per melee and no combat bonuses. Heightened state feels like twice the effort; deep sleep 10+ hours and a large meal afterward is common.

notes: Addiction may require role-played intervention. Kicking addiction can involve counseling or major unaided success with affected skills.

prerequisites.summary: At least one feature from the Artisan or Hobbyist table.

name: Mystic Amplifier
description: Increases the Nightbane's P.P.E. reserve for a limited duration. Upon activation, remaining P.P.E. is doubled; expended P.P.E. is subtracted from the added points first. The bonus P.P.E. is freely available to anyone in range who can draw on it — including enemy magic-users. While amplified, the Nightbane becomes a transitional place of power: spell casters within 20 feet (6.1 m) gain +50% range and duration on incantations, as do the user's own spells and Talents. Beings sensitive to magic can track the user from an additional 500 feet (152 m) away.

enhancement.summary: Ten P.P.E. per 50% P.P.E. bonus.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per 50% P.P.E. bonus (maximum 150% amplification).

limitations.otherLimitations: Maximum P.P.E. amplification is 150%. Bonuses to magic range, duration, and damage do not stack with other Talents, abilities, ley lines, or celestial alignments — use the more powerful effect.

ranges.summary: Self, but the added P.P.E. can be drawn on at twice the normal distance.

areaOfEffect.summary: Magic amplification affects a 50 foot (15.2 m) radius per 50% P.P.E. bonus. Added P.P.E. can be drawn at twice the normal distance.

duration.summary: 1D4 melee rounds +1 melee round per level of experience.

notes: Spell casters within 20 feet (6.1 m) of the amplified Nightbane gain +50% range and duration on incantations.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one Mythical Creature, Fantasy, or Angelic Morphus trait.

name: Nature's Fury
description: One of the most destructive Talents known — calls on nature to carve a swath of devastation via localized natural disasters. Narrower than Storm Maker but offers blizzards, earthquakes/landslides, flash floods/tsunamis, thunderstorms, volcanic eruptions, and wildfires (see Dead Reign: Hell Followed for stats). The Nightbane is immune to the destruction but becomes a priority target for enemies, Nightlords, Avatars, Night Princes/Princesses, Hound Masters, and NSB agents. Good or selfish characters are unlikely to use this when innocents are at risk; acting against alignment drops it one step. Use in populated areas invites faction wrath.

limitations.otherLimitations: Disaster type must fit the area (e.g. no blizzards in Death Valley summer). No effect on the Astral Plane or Dreamstream. Origin is magic but damage dealt is not magical. Activating requires a 1D4 minute trance communing with nature — if interrupted, P.P.E. is lost and activation fails.

ranges.summary: Localized to a primary 1/2 mile (0.8 km) radius, secondary effects felt for an additional 1D4 miles (1.6-6.4 km).

duration.summary: 15 minutes per level of the Talent user's experience, or until slain or canceled.

notes: Primary effects in a 1/2 mile (0.8 km) radius; secondary effects (half damage and penalties) in an additional 1D4 miles (1.6–6.4 km). Disaster-specific damage guidelines in Dark Designs pp. 94–95.

prerequisites.summary: Level 7 or greater and a Morphus feature from the Mineral, Plant Life, or Superbeing table.

name: Necrosis
description: Inflicts grievous damage on living flesh by accelerating natural decay. The target must have unhealed wounds or a stigmata — recent injuries or wounds still healing from days or weeks prior; not old scars or missing limbs. Failed saves necrotize wounds and surrounding tissue. Biomechanical, Plant, and Alien morphuses suffer half damage and penalties; Mineral morphuses are immune. Nightbane and other regenerators temporarily lose healing for five minutes per level of the Talent user (psionic/magical healing still works).

enhancement.summary: Every two P.P.E. points spent inflicts 1D6 damage (S.D.C. then Hit Points).

ppe.notes: Every two P.P.E. spent inflicts 1D6 damage up to maximum 1D6×10 (10D6). P.P.E. amount selected BEFORE the victim saves; full cost spent whether save succeeds or fails.

limitations.otherLimitations: Living creatures only. Wampyrs and Nightbane with Artificial Appearance, Biomechanical, or Substance Abuse features are +2 to save. Maximum damage 1D6×10. Each wound may only be affected once, but different wounds on the same target can be attacked separately. A pre-existing wound, cut, scratch, bullet wound, or bite must exist before use.

ranges.summary: Touch or up to 10 feet (3.1 m) per level of experience; line of sight required.

duration.summary: Instant effect and damage with lingering penalties.

save.summary: Save vs magic 15 or greater.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: 1D6 damage per P.P.E. point spent (S.D.C. then Hit Points). Feverish: -2 attacks per melee; combat bonuses, Spd, and skills halved for 1D6 melee rounds; -3 to save vs disease for 1D4 hours. Regeneration shut off for 5 minutes per Talent-user level.
save.effectsOnSuccessfulSave: No damage or attack loss; heal/regenerate normally, but -1 all combat rolls and -5% skills for 1D6 melee rounds.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one feature from the Undead, Nightmare, Pestilence, or Stigmata table.

name: Nightshade
description: Releases a chemical scent that puts those who fail a save into a deep sleep for 2D4+2 minutes. Even successful saves inflict penalties (-1 attack, -2 combat rolls, -10% skills).

ppe.notes: Eleven P.P.E. to activate with immediate effect.

limitations.otherLimitations: If someone under the effects of this Talent is attacked, they immediately awaken and can defend with full bonuses.

ranges.summary: 20 foot (6 m) radius around the Nightbane, +5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience

duration.summary: Failed save: comatose sleep up to 2D4+2 minutes. Successful save: penalties last for the exposure.

save.summary: Save vs non-lethal poison 16 or higher. Failed save: deep sleep. Successful save: -1 attack per melee, -2 on all combat rolls, -10% to all skills.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Plant Life or Extraterrestrial characteristic.

name: No Rest
description: Like All-Nighter, enables long stretches without sleep and without chemical stimulants. On self, eliminates sleep-deprivation effects for one day per level, keeps the user fully awake and alert, grants +1 Perception, and +5 to save vs sedatives and sleep-inducing powers. As an attack (unlike All-Nighter), may be used on unwilling targets who fail save vs magic 14+ — they fatigue normally but cannot sleep. Cumulative penalties per 24 hours without sleep; after 72 hours regeneration halves; after 120 hours regeneration stops; after 168 hours daily insanity saves; 13 days triggers Random Insanity Table. Effects can be canceled by the user at any distance; sleep magic/psionics force a save to counteract. Usable in a Dream Pool to awaken the dreamer and expel intruders without a save.

enhancement.summary: +3 extra P.P.E. to raise the saving throw by one point (max 15).
enhancement.stepDefinition: +1 to the target's saving throw difficulty

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. per use. +3 extra P.P.E. to raise the saving throw by one point (maximum 15).

limitations.otherLimitations: Effects continue even when the user shifts to Facade. Self-use removes physical need for rest for days equal to M.E.; beyond that, daily save vs insanity (temporary insanities clear after 8+ hours sleep). Long-term self-use or offensive use on others takes a psychological toll.

ranges.summary: Self and one other by touch with good intentions. Touch or 30 feet (9.1 m) when used on one victim as an attack.

duration.summary: 24 hours per level of experience.

save.summary: Unwilling targets save vs magic 14 or better (+3 P.P.E. per +1 to save difficulty, maximum 15).
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Target cannot sleep. Per 24 hours without sleep: -1 melee attack, -1 Perception/initiative/all combat/saves, -10% all skills (cumulative). After 72 hours regeneration halved; after 120 hours regeneration stops; after 168 hours daily insanity save 16+; 13 days roll on Random Insanity Table. Penalties halved and insanities clear after 10+ hours sleep; full restore after two consecutive 8-hour sleep periods (50% chance one insanity remains if deprivation lasted 10+ days).

notes: While active on self: +5 to save vs sedatives and sleep-inducing powers. Effects can be canceled by the user at any distance. Usable in a Dream Pool to awaken the dreamer and expel intruders without a save.

prerequisites.summary: Level 3 or greater and at least one trait from the Occupation, Soldier, Ancient Warrior, or Superbeing table.

name: Overdrive
description: Drastically increases maximum speed of the user or conveyance to 500 mph (800 kph). The Morphus reconfigures — aerodynamic shape, thrusters, rocket roar or supercharger whine. +2 Perception when looking ahead for trouble and +2 dodge while in motion. Vehicle/animal pilots gain +30% to appropriate Piloting and halve high-speed maneuver penalties. Objects under 10 lbs (4.5 kg) are harmlessly knocked aside; collisions over that inflict 1D4×10 to user/vehicle and target and cancel Overdrive. No physical strain or surface damage at speed unless crash; any flat surface including water works like dry pavement. Supports flight, swimming, running, or driving. On lapse, speed returns to normal within a half mile (0.8 km) in 2–3 seconds.

enhancement.summary: +8 P.P.E. per additional 100 miles (160 km) traveled.

ppe.notes: Sixteen P.P.E. per 100 miles (160 km) traveled. Double P.P.E. for vehicles with up to 4 passengers or 800 lbs (360 kg) cargo; triple for vehicles carrying 20 passengers or 10 tons of cargo.

limitations.otherLimitations: Combat largely limited to dodging, firing ahead, or ramming. Vehicle altitude/depth limits, S.D.C., and engine requirements unchanged. All-or-nothing speed: 500 mph (800 kph) or normal speed — nothing in between without canceling.

ranges.summary: Self, riding animal or vehicle by touch.

duration.summary: Special — lasts for distance traveled; see P.P.E. costs per 100 miles.

physicalCombatStatModifiers.notes: +30% to appropriate Piloting skill; halve penalties for high-speed maneuvers when piloting a vehicle or riding animal.

prerequisites.summary: Level 4 or greater and at least one Morphus feature from the Biomechanical, Aquatic Biomech, Superbeing, or Sci-Fi table.

name: Pariah's Mantle
description: Draws the ire of others to the user — useful for distraction or protecting weaker allies. Selected targets in range must save 15 or better (including M.E. bonuses) or be overcome with revulsion and violent hatred. They threaten, insult, and assault within 1D4 melee rounds (immediately if they already disliked the user). Bystanders focus on the pariah and assailants; stealthy allies gain cover — relevant Perception Rolls are -6.

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per minute of use.

limitations.otherLimitations: Anyone affected who is harmed in any way is immediately released and immune for 24 hours. Good alignment targets get +2 to save; total pacifists get +5.

ranges.summary: 20 feet (6.1 m) +5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: One minute per P.P.E. spent.

save.summary: Save vs magic 15 or better (M.E. bonuses apply; good alignment +2, pacifists +5).
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Revulsion and violent hatred; threats, insults, and physical assault within 1D4 melee rounds (immediate if already hostile).

notes: Bystanders and observers focus on the pariah; allies on stealth/Prowl tasks enjoy -6 to enemy Perception Rolls that might notice them.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Morphus feature from the Disproportion, Unusual Facial Feature, or Clown table.

name: Passing the Torch
description: Turn ordinary mortals into Nightbane temporarily — or, in rare approved cases, permanently. Temporary use: a willing recipient undergoes the Becoming in an exact copy of the Talent user's Morphus (or GM may assign a new Morphus). They count as Nightbane for targeted effects, with Mirror Walk, sense fellow Nightbane, and Talents copied from the user or selected as level 1. Shock penalties apply even when forewarned. While active, the Talent user is limited to Facade form and reverts immediately on activation. Ends if the recipient is slain (Morphus returns; user knows) or when canceled. Permanent Passing the Torch requires willing certainty, GM approval, at least 20 P.P.E. available, and kills the Talent user — the recipient becomes a full Nightbane retaining Facade attributes, skills, and level.

ppe.notes: Twelve P.P.E. per hour for temporary Morphus lending. Permanent Passing the Torch requires at least 20 P.P.E. available, GM approval, and kills the Talent user; the new Nightbane permanently spends 12 P.P.E. from the inherited pool.

limitations.otherLimitations: Willing targets only — unwilling or incapacitated targets automatically save. Only mortal races without psychic abilities. While temporary Passing the Torch is active, the Talent user is limited to Facade form.

ranges.summary: Touch or 10 feet (3 m).

duration.summary: Temporary: paid per hour while Morphus is lent. Permanent transformation is irrevocable.

penaltiesOnAffectedCharacters.notes: Temporary Nightbane: exhilarating and disorienting Becoming shock even when the recipient knows what to expect.

notes: Permanent torch-passing is an exceedingly rare deathbed sacrifice. New permanent Nightbane inherits Morphus abilities and P.P.E. (minus 12 permanently expended) but keeps own Facade stats and knowledge.

prerequisites.summary: Level 7 or greater and one or more features from the Superbeing, Athlete, or Stigmata tables.

name: Payday
description: Similar to Pocket Change, conjures money from thin air. Ten physical dollars (or local equivalent) appear per 1 P.P.E. spent — real currency that passes forgery inspection and works at registers and vending machines. The Nightbane chooses coin or note denominations up to the P.P.E. spent, in the currency of the country they are in. Created currency lasts longer than Pocket Change but vanishes from pockets, tills, and vaults when the duration expires. Alternatively, the user may conjure 100 dollars per P.E. point expended (24 hours to recover each P.E. point; adjust bonuses and saves accordingly).

ppe.notes: One P.P.E. per 10 dollars (or local equivalent) created. Alternatively, conjure 100 dollars per P.E. point expended (same duration; 24 hours to recover each P.E. point).

limitations.otherLimitations: Must picture the currency to create it — familiarity with that money type is required (e.g. a Nightbane in Europe needs to know the Euro and/or have an example in hand).

ranges.summary: Self

duration.summary: One hour per level of experience.

createdObjects.name: Physical Currency
createdObjects.summary: Ten dollars (or local equivalent) per 1 P.P.E., or 100 dollars per P.E. point expended.

prerequisites.summary: Level 3 or greater and at least one feature from the Occupation, Athlete, or Hobbyist table.

name: Rallying Cry
description: A supernatural battlefield or locker-room rallying cry that lifts allies and supporters. The cry may be historical, athletic, or pop-cultural — effects are the same. Allies in range gain +2 initiative, +1 strike/parry/dodge, +1 to all saving throws, and +5% to all skills. Allies under demoralizing or manipulating effects (failed Horror Factor, psionic mind control, etc.) immediately reroll the save with the new bonus. Optionally, expend double P.P.E. on specific allies so they reroll against negative effects or penalty saves (14+ including applicable bonuses for non-save penalties such as injury modifiers).

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per Rallying Cry. Optionally expend 12 P.P.E. (double) on specific allies for penalty-removal rerolls.

limitations.otherLimitations: User must have a general idea of each target's presence and location. Bonuses do NOT accumulate with multiple uses.

ranges.summary: Every ally or supporter within 10 feet (3.1 m) radius per level of experience, or specific people within range and line of sight.

areaOfEffect.summary: 10 foot (3.1 m) radius per level of experience.

duration.summary: Cry is instantaneous; bonuses last one melee round per level of the Talent user's experience.

notes: Allies affected: +2 initiative, +1 strike/parry/dodge, +1 all saves, +5% skills. Double-P.P.E. option grants reroll vs existing penalties or save 14+ vs non-save penalties for the duration.

retroactiveSaveGrants.summary: Allies under demoralizing or manipulating effects immediately reroll with Rallying Cry bonuses.
retroactiveSaveGrants.versusPreExistingEffectSummary: Optional double P.P.E. targets specific allies for rerolls vs penalties or negative effects.

prerequisites.summary: At least one feature from the Ancient Warrior, Athlete, or Soldier table.

name: Revolting Spit
description: Inspired by caustic dinosaur venom, the Nightbane secretes mild organic acid held in the mouth for a bite or spit. Each use counts as two melee attacks (conjure, then bite or spit). Bite deals normal Morphus damage plus acid. Spit: called shot to the face requires natural roll 14+ (no strike bonuses); may be parried with a shield or hand/forearm. Acid does 1D4 damage initially plus +1 per round for 1D4 melee rounds. Face hit with failed save vs non-lethal poison 13+: blindness and severe fumes penalties. Water neutralizes acid immediately; washing takes 1D4 melee rounds.

ppe.notes: Six P.P.E. per spit projection; counts as two melee attacks.

cannotAffect: inorganic materials (glass, ceramics, crystal, stone, plastic, metal)
Nightbane in full environmental suit or armor
limitations.otherLimitations: Gas mask covering eyes, nose, and mouth prevents penalties. Goggles/visors prevent blindness until removed; fumes still apply. Water neutralizes acid. Clothing and natural fabrics take damage; plants and wood take damage.

ranges.summary: Bite or 10 feet (3.1 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Instant attack; damage and penalties last 1D4 melee rounds or until washed away.

damage: 1D4 initially, +1 per melee round for next 1D4 rounds; double to Nightlords and Nightlands denizens; double (half to Hit Points) on exposed cuts of 3+ damage or vulnerable tissue

save.summary: Save vs non-lethal poison 13 or higher on face hit; called shot natural 14+ required to hit the face.
save.effectsOnSuccessfulSave: -1 melee attack, -1 all combat rolls, -2 Perception, -10% skills (eyes water; fumes hinder breathing).
save.effectsOnFailedSave: 1D4 damage; temporarily blinded (-10 all combat rolls, -80% sight-based skills) plus fumes: -2 melee attacks, -2 combat, -2 Perception, -20% skills for 1D4 melee rounds.

notes: Acid may be held in the mouth until used (prevents speech; may drool). Useful for blinding surveillance cameras until washed off.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: 1D4 damage initially, plus +1 point of additional damage per each of the next 1D4 melee rounds. Double damage to Nightlords, their minions, and Denizens of the Nightlands. Double damage (half direct to HP) on exposed cuts or vulnerable tissue.
attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Counts as two melee attacks (one to conjure, one to bite or spit).

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: A Called Shot of 14 or better (Natural, unmodified roll) is required to hit the face and impose blindness.

statusAlterations.summary: If hit in the face and victim saves: Eyes water and fumes make breathing difficult. -1 melee attack, -1 to all combat rolls, -2 on Perception Rolls, and -10% to all skills.

If hit in the face and victim fails to save: Temporarily blinded and overwhelmed by noxious fumes. -10 to all combat rolls, -80% to touch-based skills (0% if sight is required). Additionally, fumes cause -2 melee attacks, -2 to all combat rolls, -2 on Perception Rolls, and -20% to skill performance.

prerequisites.summary: Level 2 or greater and one or more features from the Dinosaur, Snake, Extraterrestrial, or Mythical Creature tables.

name: Rigor Mortis
description: Mimics rigor mortis — stiffening after death. On self or willing targets (Facade or Morphus): appears completely dead with no detectable heartbeat, breathing, or brain activity on modern medical equipment; suspended animation (5% oxygen, no food/water, drugs/poisons cease metabolizing) while senses remain active. First Aid -90%, Paramedic -70%, Medical Doctor -50% to detect the state. Willing feign-death can be canceled at will when attacked — only ends if the user chooses unless life is in jeopardy, then they revive without penalties and may surprise attackers. On unwilling targets (Morphus only): failed save vs magic 12+ stiffens muscles; P.S., P.P., Spd, attacks, combat bonuses, and skills reduced 20–80% by P.P.E. spent.

activationTiers.summary: Feign death on self or willing participant
Debilitate unwilling target: 5 P.P.E. per -20% penalty imposed (80% maximum)

ppe.notes: Three P.P.E. on self or willing feign death. Five P.P.E. per -20% penalty on unwilling victims (80% max — most victims barely move with 1–2 attacks).

limitations.otherLimitations: When feigning death willingly, effects cancel only when the user chooses — being stabbed does not automatically end the Talent unless they want to break the pretense.

ranges.summary: Touch or up to 20 feet (6.1 m) +5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.summary: Opponents: one minute per level. Self or willing feign death: one hour per level.

save.summary: Unwilling targets save vs magic 12 or higher.
save.effectsOnFailedSave: Muscles stiffen; P.S., P.P., Spd, attacks per melee, combat bonuses, and skill performance reduced 20–80% based on P.P.E. spent.

duration.durationSummary: One hour per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Appears completely dead. Heartbeat and breathing stop, temperature cools. Uses 5% of normal oxygen, requires no food/water, ceases metabolizing drugs/poison. Still able to see, hear, and smell. Can be canceled at any time.

enhancement.summary: Every 5 P.P.E. imposes a -20% penalty, up to a maximum of 80%.
enhancement.stepDefinition: -20% penalty to physical stats and actions

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Touch or up to 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.durationSummary: One minute per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Reduces P.S., P.P., Spd, attacks per melee, combat bonuses, and skill performance by 20-80% based on P.P.E. spent.

prerequisites.summary: Level 4 or greater and at least one feature from the Undead, Pestilence, or Unnatural Limbs table.

name: Seedling
description: Allows the user to plant a seed imbued with a fragment of their life essence. It grows into a tree reflecting their Morphus form, extending their lifespan and serving as a safe haven and P.P.E. battery.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E., one Hit Point, and one P.E. attribute point are permanently placed into the Seedling when it is planted. Communing with or hiding within a Seedling has no cost.

attributeBurn.summary: One Hit Point and one P.E. attribute point are permanently lost when the Seedling is planted.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only ONE seedling can be planted for the lifetime of the Nightbane.

duration.durationSummary: Lives for decades or until destroyed. The tree lives 1D6x10 years after the Nightbane dies.

comaHitPointBuffer.summary: Provides 1D6+1 extra Hit Points per level of experience that are secretly added to the Nightbane's total when dropping below zero.
comaHitPointBuffer.durationWhenTriggered: Remains in a healing coma state until regenerated to 1D6+10 Hit Points above zero.

statusAlterations.summary: Lifespan extended by 1D6x10+60 years. +30% to save vs coma/death. Can breathe without air for 2 minutes per P.E. attribute point. Immune to carbon dioxide.

createdObjects.objectSummary: A magical tree tied to the creator's essence.

growthStages.growthStageSummary: Sprouts into a small sapling (1D4+4 feet tall).
growthStages.abilitiesAtStage: Nightbane can see, hear, and feel everything within a 20 foot (6.1 m) radius by concentrating for 1 minute.

Nightbane can meld with and hide inside the tree. Heals at double rate, needs no food/water, gains awareness in 200 ft radius. Tree acts as a P.P.E. battery containing 5D6+6 P.P.E. accessible up to 1 mile away.

growthStages.growthStageSummary: Doubles in size a final time.
growthStages.abilitiesAtStage: Gains full lifespan extension, coma/death bonuses, and secret Hit Point buffer.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one feature from the Angelic, Alien Shape, Mythical Creature, Plant Life, or Unnatural Limbs table.

name: Shadow Swarm
description: Creates a horrific illusion of a swarm of vermin moving about in the shadows that corrals targets or causes them to flee.

cannotAffect: Brightly lit areas (the swarm cannot enter light)

duration.durationSummary: One melee round (15 seconds) + 8 seconds per level of experience.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Horror Factor 15 for the average person, 12 for the typical Nightbane, 17 for those with a phobia of vermin, darkness, or shadows.

statusAlterations.summary: Victims who step in or are caught by the swarm vanish into shadows for one melee round + 10 seconds per level. They experience peace and reappear unhurt at the same location.

prerequisites.summary: Level 3 or greater and at least one feature from the Animal, Pestilence, or Undead table.

name: Shadows of Despair
description: Manipulates the emotions of victims, plunging them into the depths of despair and hopelessness.

ppe.notes: Twelve P.P.E. per 1D4+1 melee rounds, or 120 P.P.E. for 1D4+2 days.

limitations.otherLimitations: Cannot make someone suicidal. Successful save grants 24-hour immunity.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Two targets by touch or one target up to 100 feet (30.5 m) away; line of sight required.

duration.durationSummary: 1D4+1 melee rounds per 12 P.P.E. spent, or 1D4+2 days if 120 P.P.E. is spent.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Magical, supernatural, and alien beings get +5 to save.

statusAlterations.summary: -6 on Perception Rolls, initiative, and save vs Horror Factor. -3 on saving throws and all other combat bonuses. -25% on skill rolls (-50% if life and death). Interrogators gain +20%. Remaining combat bonuses halved. Victim fatigues twice as fast and heals at half rate. 60% chance to flee/surrender at half HP unless a M.E. save of 16 is made.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Morphus feature from the Nightmare, Pestilence, Substance Abuse, or Victim table.

name: Share the Needle
description: Recreates some of the worst effects of drug addiction and withdrawal to debilitate an enemy, or can be used to instantly sober someone up.

ppe.notes: Five P.P.E. per 1D6 minutes or 20 P.P.E. for 1D4x10 minutes. Only one symptom can be inflicted at a time.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only one symptom can be inflicted at a time. Penalties and effects are at half when applied to supernatural or magical creatures.

duration.durationSummary: 1D6 minutes per 5 P.P.E., or 1D4x10 minutes per 20 P.P.E.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: Supernatural or magical creatures suffer half penalties. A save of 12 is used when resisting the 'Sober Up' effect.

statusAlterations.summary: Anxiety/Restlessness/Wired: -20% to all skills, -1 to Perception, -3 to save vs Horror Factor.
Cold Sweats: Highly uncomfortable, -5% on all skills. Tracking victim by scent is +15%.
Dizziness: When moving faster than Spd 4 or fighting: -2 attacks, -4 to all combat rolls, -40% to skills. Balance-related skills are impossible.
Fatigue: Spd, attacks, and combat bonuses halved. -10% to skills. Fatigues twice as fast.
Headache: -1 to initiative and Perception, -20% to skills, -1 to all saving throws.
Muscle Cramps: P.S., P.P., Spd, and all combat bonuses reduced by half.
Nausea/Vomiting: Moving faster than half Spd or strenuous activity has a 0-75% chance per melee of causing vomiting. Vomiting uses all but one melee attack and costs initiative.
Tremors: P.P., Spd, and skill performance reduced by half. -6 to strike with ranged/thrown weapons.
Sober Up: Alleviates drug/alcohol highs within 1D4 melee rounds and reduces subsequent hangover/withdrawal symptoms by half.

prerequisites.summary: Level 5 or greater and at least one trait from the Substance Abuse or Victim table.

name: Sideshow
description: Grants the user the remarkable ability to move like a trained acrobat or gymnast while in and out of combat.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. to activate and 6 P.P.E. to sustain each additional minute.

duration.durationSummary: One minute per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: +2 to parry and dodge, +3 to roll with punch/fall/impact. No strike penalties applied for attacking on the move. +2 to strike with kick attacks, +2 to disarm.

prerequisites.summary: Level 2 or greater and at least one Morphus feature from the Clown, Athlete, or Disproportion table.

name: Spelljack
description: Hijack control of a spell cast near the Nightbane or on a target in range. The Nightbane may cancel it, keep it, alter duration/range, or seize control of Living Spells.

baseActivation.summary: One quarter the P.P.E. cost of the spell being jacked (rounded up)

ppe.notes: Twenty P.P.E. to acquire permanently. Hijack cost scales with the spell's P.P.E. cost. Living Spells that fail a save of 15 fall under control for 1D4 minutes per level.

limitations.otherLimitations: Only affects spell magic — not rituals, wards, circles, runes, or magic weapons. Instant-duration spells require winning initiative vs the caster (Nightbane adds initiative bonus).

ranges.summary: Caster or spell target within 25 feet (7.6 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience

save.summary: Living Spells save vs 15 or are Spelljacked for 1D4 minutes per level.

prerequisites.summary: At least one feature from the Fantasy or Infernal table.

name: Summoner
description: Summons demonic minions (Sub-Demons, Lesser Demons, or a Greater Demon) from magical portals to serve the user.

activationTiers.summary: One Sub-Demon (up to three may be summoned)
One Lesser Demon (up to two may be summoned)

enhancement.summary: Strength (+5 Supernatural P.S. and +50 S.D.C.), Flight, Stealth, Biting Jaws, Armament, or Firepower — 5 P.P.E. per demon per enhancement, maximum three enhancements.
enhancement.stepDefinition: One enhancement on one summoned demon

ppe.notes: Twenty P.P.E. per Sub-Demon, 40 per Lesser Demon, 80 per Greater Demon. Each enhancement adds 5 P.P.E. per demon (limit three enhancements total per summoning).

limitations.otherLimitations: Up to three Sub-Demons, two Lesser Demons, or one Greater Demon per activation. Minions are completely loyal for the duration, then disappear.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Portals open up to 30 feet (9.1 m) away. Demons can travel up to 1,000 feet (305 m) away per level of experience.

duration.durationSummary: Sub-Demons: 40 minutes. Lesser Demons: 20 minutes. Greater Demon: 10 minutes.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one Infernal, Fantasy, or Nightmare Morphus trait.

name: Swarm Self
description: Causes the Nightbane's body to separate into 2D4x10 tiny animals of the same animal type as the character. The Nightbane controls the swarm and is aware of everything they do or see.

prerequisites.summary: At least one characteristic from the Animal or Insect Form Table.

enhancement.summary: An additional 10 P.P.E. for each additional two minutes.

ppe.notes: Ten P.P.E. for the first two minutes and an additional 10 P.P.E. for each additional two minutes.

ranges.summary: Self; once a swarm, the range is unrestricted.

duration.summary: Two minutes

swarmMechanics.collectivePoolSummary: Each swarm member has 5 S.D.C. and 1 Hit Point. A swarm of 10 critters will have 50 S.D.C. and 10 Hit Points. Attacks on the swarm have no modifiers to hit.

reversionDamageCarryover.summary: The hit point (not S.D.C.) damage taken by the swarm carries over to the Nightbane's hit points after the swarm is destroyed or recalled.

swarmMechanics.notes: In combat, they do 1 point of S.D.C. damage individually. If the swarm takes more than half its S.D.C. damage, damage is reduced to 2D6. When all S.D.C. is depleted, only two swarmlings remain. At least two swarmlings must be present for the Nightbane to rebuild himself; most retreat and reform when reduced to 10 or 15. If all are destroyed, the Nightbane is slain.

name: The Taint
description: Anything the Nightbane touches decays. Metal rusts, cloth tatters. Living beings take magical necrotic damage that heals at half speed. Inflicts penalties to medical skills used to treat it.

enhancement.summary: +2 P.P.E. per additional 1D6 damage (up to 6 P.P.E. / 3D6 per level of experience).

ppe.notes: Nine P.P.E. to activate plus 2 P.P.E. per 1D6 damage. Maximum 6 P.P.E. (3D6) per level on damage. P.P.E. is spent even if the attack misses.

limitations.otherLimitations: Touch attack — can be dodged or parried (parrying object takes damage). Successful save vs magic: one-third damage. Natural A.R. does not protect supernatural targets. Does 30% more damage to Night Princes and Nightlords. +2 Horror Factor if the attack continues. Victims below zero Hit Points are -10% to save vs coma/death. P.P.E. is spent even if the attack misses.

ranges.summary: Touch

duration.summary: Taint touch lasts one melee round per activation.

save.summary: Save vs magic; on success suffer only one-third damage.

prerequisites.summary: At least one Undead or Victim table characteristic.

name: The Ties that Bind
description: Creates ensnaring chains, razor wire, or tendrils that shoot out to entangle, damage, and torture an opponent.

enhancement.summary: +1 P.P.E. per additional point of tendril P.S. (base 16, maximum 24). Agony pulses cost 2 P.P.E. per tendril used.

ppe.notes: Fifteen P.P.E. per tendril created, plus 1 P.P.E. per additional point of tendril P.S. (maximum 24). Each agony pulse costs 2 P.P.E. per tendril.

limitations.otherLimitations: Targets must be visible and within range. Victims with Supernatural P.S. equal to or greater than the combined strength of entangling tendrils may attempt to break free (two melee actions; 2D6 damage per tendril whether freed or not). Tendrils may be severed by 26 S.D.C. in a single attack; only vulnerable to spells, psionics, Talents, magical weapons, and Supernatural beings.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: 20 feet (6.1 m) + 5 feet (1.5 m) per level of experience.

duration.durationSummary: One minute (4 melee rounds) per level of experience.

attacksAndDamage.baseDamageSummary: Tendril strike does 4D6 damage (6D6 against Nightlords/minions). Constriction does half damage (2D6) and cannot be defended against. Tendrils have P.S. 16 and +3 to strike/entangle.
attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Breaking free takes two melee actions.

savingThrows.bonusToSaveSummary: P.E. bonuses apply. -2 penalty to save for each tendril beyond the first when agony pulsing simultaneously.

statusAlterations.summary: One tendril imposes -2 to subsequent combat rolls. Two tendrils double this to -4. Three or more prevent any defensive action except parrying with a free limb at -6.
Throat constriction (requires Called Shot 16+ unmodified): Cuts off airway. Unconsciousness in 1D6+4 melees, coma after 1D4+2 mins, death in 2D4 mins.
Agony Pulse (Failed Save): Victim loses two attacks and cannot use spells, psionics, skills, or do anything but scream for the melee round.
Agony Pulse (Successful Save): Victim loses one melee attack and is -1 to save against the next agony pulse.

prerequisites.summary: Level 6 or greater and at least one feature from the Infernal, Nightmare, or Stigmata table.

name: Too Much Stereo
description: Turns the Nightbane into a stadium-quality sound system, capable of projecting voice or music at up to 150 decibels, or firing directed sonic blasts.

activationTiers.summary: Directed sonic blast (once per melee round, in addition to amplification)

ppe.notes: Two P.P.E. to amplify voice, 10 P.P.E. to blare music. Each directed sonic blast requires an additional 5 P.P.E. and can only be fired once per melee round.

limitations.otherLimitations: Targets may not be selected individually for the AoE effects; everyone within range is affected. Highly likely to attract authorities and Nightlords' minions.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Clearly heard up to 2 miles (3.2 km) away outdoors.

duration.durationSummary: Up to one minute per level of experience.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Clearly heard up to 2 miles (3.2 km) away outdoors.
rangeAndArea.areaOfEffectSummary: 500 feet (152.4 m) radius for debilitating effects.

duration.durationSummary: Up to one minute per level of experience.

statusAlterations.summary: Without hearing protection: Must shout to communicate (5 ft range max), -3 on Perception Rolls (-6 if requiring hearing), -2 on initiative, -2 to parry and disarm, -4 to dodge, and -15% on skill performance.

attacksAndDamage.actionCost: Can only be fired once per melee round, and counts as one melee attack.

rangeAndArea.rangeSummary: Column-shaped (600 feet / 183 m long, 6 feet / 1.8 m in diameter) OR Cone-shaped (300 feet / 91.4 m long, 45-90 degree arc).
